=============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- Add position column to deals for Kanban ordering\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nALTER TABLE deals\\\\\\\\n  ADD COLUMN position INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWITH ranked AS (\\\\\\\\n  SELECT\\\\\\\\n    id,\\\\\\\\n    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (\\\\\\\\n      PARTITION BY pipeline_id, stage_id\\\\\\\\n      ORDER BY created_at DESC\\\\\\\\n    ) AS pos\\\\\\\\n  FROM deals\\\\\\\\n)\\\\\\\\nUPDATE deals d\\\\\\\\nSET position = ranked.pos\\\\\\\\nFROM ranked\\\\\\\\nWHERE d.id = ranked.id;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_deals_pipeline_stage_position ON deals(pipeline_id, stage_id, position);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":23}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"a17d13dc-aa9c-4383-a491-0b1063e66f02\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"e3880c29-3c58-4a82-aacb-bd13a79964e2\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-02-17T10:18:47.023Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"pendingToolCalls\\\":[],\\\"turns\\\":[],\\\"previousWorkspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"fileStates\\\":{},\\\"summaryArchives\\\":[],\\\"turnTimings\\\":[],\\\"fileStatesV2\\\":{},\\\"subagentStates\\\":{},\\\"selfSummaryCount\\\":0,\\\"readPaths\\\":[]},\\\"text\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"toolFormerData\\\":{\\\"toolCallId\\\":\\\"tool_ea10e04c-e536-4cdb-85d1-14135f9a600\\\",\\\"toolIndex\\\":0,\\\"modelCallId\\\":\\\"tool_ea10e04c-e536-4cdb-85d1-14135f9a600\\\",\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"read_file_v2\\\",\\\"rawArgs\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"migrations/1737675200000_email_sequences.sql\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"tool\\\":40,\\\"params\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"targetFile\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"migrations/1737675200000_email_sequences.sql\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"charsLimit\\\\\\\":1000000,\\\\\\\"effectiveUri\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"migrations/1737675200000_email_sequences.sql\\\\\\\"}\\\",\\\"result\\\":\\\"{\\\\\\\"contents\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- DNA Marketing Engine - E-Mail Sequences Migration\\\\\\\\n-- PostgreSQL 15+\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- EMAIL SEQUENCES\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE email_sequences (\\\\\\\\n    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    name            VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    description     TEXT,\\\\\\\\n    trigger_event   VARCHAR(50),              -- z.B. 'stage_enter', 'lead_created', 'form_submit'\\\\\\\\n    trigger_config  JSONB DEFAULT '{}',       -- Zusätzliche Trigger-Konfiguration\\\\\\\\n    is_active       BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,\\\\\\\\n    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- EMAIL SEQUENCE STEPS\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE email_sequence_steps (\\\\\\\\n    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    sequence_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_sequences(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\n    position        INTEGER NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    delay_days      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    delay_hours     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    subject         VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    body_html       TEXT NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    body_text       TEXT,\\\\\\\\n    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    UNIQUE(sequence_id, position)\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- EMAIL SEQUENCE ENROLLMENTS\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE email_sequence_enrollments (\\\\\\\\n    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    lead_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES leads(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\n    sequence_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_sequences(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\n    current_step        INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    status              VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',  -- active, paused, completed, unsubscribed\\\\\\\\n    enrolled_at         TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    last_email_sent_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    next_email_due_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,                   -- Wann die nächste E-Mail gesendet werden soll\\\\\\\\n    completed_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    unsubscribed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    metadata            JSONB DEFAULT '{}',\\\\\\\\n    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    UNIQUE(lead_id, sequence_id)\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- EMAIL TRACKING (für Opens und Clicks)\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE email_tracking (\\\\\\\\n    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    enrollment_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_sequence_enrollments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\n    step_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_sequence_steps(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\\\\\\\n    sent_at         TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    opened_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    open_count      INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    clicked_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    click_count     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    bounced_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    bounce_reason   TEXT,\\\\\\\\n    unsubscribed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    metadata        JSONB DEFAULT '{}'\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- INDEXES\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Email sequences indexes\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_sequences_active ON email_sequences(is_active) WHERE is_active = TRUE;\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_sequences_trigger ON email_sequences(trigger_event);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Email sequence steps indexes\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_sequence_steps_sequence ON email_sequence_steps(sequence_id, position);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Email sequence enrollments indexes\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_enrollments_lead ON email_sequence_enrollments(lead_id);\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_enrollments_sequence ON email_sequence_enrollments(sequence_id);\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_enrollments_status ON email_sequence_enrollments(status) WHERE status = 'active';\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_enrollments_next_due ON email_sequence_enrollments(next_email_due_at) \\\\\\\\n    WHERE status = 'active' AND next_email_due_at IS NOT NULL;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Email tracking indexes\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_tracking_enrollment ON email_tracking(enrollment_id);\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_tracking_step ON email_tracking(step_id);\\\\\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_email_tracking_sent ON email_tracking(sent_at DESC);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- TRIGGERS\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Auto-update updated_at on email_sequences\\\\\\\\nCREATE TRIGGER trigger_email_sequences_updated_at\\\\\\\\n    BEFORE UPDATE ON email_sequences\\\\\\\\n    FOR EACH ROW\\\\\\\\n    EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Auto-update updated_at on email_sequence_steps\\\\\\\\nCREATE TRIGGER trigger_email_sequence_steps_updated_at\\\\\\\\n    BEFORE UPDATE ON email_sequence_steps\\\\\\\\n    FOR EACH ROW\\\\\\\\n    EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Auto-update updated_at on email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\nCREATE TRIGGER trigger_email_enrollments_updated_at\\\\\\\\n    BEFORE UPDATE ON email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\n    FOR EACH ROW\\\\\\\\n    EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- HELPER FUNCTIONS\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Calculate next email due date based on current step and delays\\\\\\\\nCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_next_email_due(\\\\\\\\n    p_enrollment_id UUID\\\\\\\\n) RETURNS TIMESTAMPTZ AS $$\\\\\\\\nDECLARE\\\\\\\\n    v_sequence_id UUID;\\\\\\\\n    v_current_step INTEGER;\\\\\\\\n    v_last_sent TIMESTAMPTZ;\\\\\\\\n    v_delay_days INTEGER;\\\\\\\\n    v_delay_hours INTEGER;\\\\\\\\n    v_next_due TIMESTAMPTZ;\\\\\\\\nBEGIN\\\\\\\\n    -- Get enrollment info\\\\\\\\n    SELECT sequence_id, current_step, COALESCE(last_email_sent_at, enrolled_at)\\\\\\\\n    INTO v_sequence_id, v_current_step, v_last_sent\\\\\\\\n    FROM email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\n    WHERE id = p_enrollment_id;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    -- Get next step delay\\\\\\\\n    SELECT delay_days, COALESCE(delay_hours, 0)\\\\\\\\n    INTO v_delay_days, v_delay_hours\\\\\\\\n    FROM email_sequence_steps\\\\\\\\n    WHERE sequence_id = v_sequence_id AND position = v_current_step + 1;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    -- If no next step found, return NULL\\\\\\\\n    IF v_delay_days IS NULL THEN\\\\\\\\n        RETURN NULL;\\\\\\\\n    END IF;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    -- Calculate next due date\\\\\\\\n    v_next_due := v_last_sent + (v_delay_days || ' days')::INTERVAL + (v_delay_hours || ' hours')::INTERVAL;\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    RETURN v_next_due;\\\\\\\\nEND;\\\\\\\\n$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Update next_email_due_at after step completion\\\\\\\\nCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_enrollment_next_due()\\\\\\\\nRETURNS TRIGGER AS $$\\\\\\\\nBEGIN\\\\\\\\n    -- Only update if current_step or last_email_sent_at changed\\\\\\\\n    IF (NEW.current_step != OLD.current_step OR NEW.last_email_sent_at != OLD.last_email_sent_at) \\\\\\\\n       AND NEW.status = 'active' THEN\\\\\\\\n        NEW.next_email_due_at := calculate_next_email_due(NEW.id);\\\\\\\\n    END IF;\\\\\\\\n    RETURN NEW;\\\\\\\\nEND;\\\\\\\\n$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCREATE TRIGGER trigger_update_enrollment_next_due\\\\\\\\n    BEFORE UPDATE ON email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\n    FOR EACH ROW\\\\\\\\n    EXECUTE FUNCTION update_enrollment_next_due();\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- COMMENTS\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON TABLE email_sequences IS 'E-Mail-Marketing-Sequenzen für automatisierte E-Mail-Ketten';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON TABLE email_sequence_steps IS 'Einzelne E-Mail-Schritte innerhalb einer Sequenz';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON TABLE email_sequence_enrollments IS 'Zuordnung von Leads zu E-Mail-Sequenzen';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON TABLE email_tracking IS 'Tracking von E-Mail-Opens, Clicks und Bounces';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON COLUMN email_sequences.trigger_event IS 'Ereignis das die Sequenz startet: stage_enter, lead_created, form_submit, manual';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON COLUMN email_sequence_steps.delay_days IS 'Verzögerung in Tagen nach dem vorherigen Schritt';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON COLUMN email_sequence_steps.delay_hours IS 'Zusätzliche Verzögerung in Stunden';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON COLUMN email_sequence_enrollments.status IS 'Status: active, paused, completed, unsubscribed';\\\\\\\\nCOMMENT ON COLUMN email_sequence_enrollments.next_email_due_at IS 'Berechneter Zeitpunkt für die nächste E-Mail';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"totalLinesInFile\\\\\\\":192}\\\"},\\\"capabilityType\\\":15}\",\"c4308fe1-b8ce-4d91-ba90-fdeb0bf199d3\":\"{\\\"_v\\\":3,\\\"type\\\":2,\\\"approximateLintErrors\\\":[],\\\"lints\\\":[],\\\"codebaseContextChunks\\\":[],\\\"commits\\\":[],\\\"pullRequests\\\":[],\\\"attachedCodeChunks\\\":[],\\\"assistantSuggestedDiffs\\\":[],\\\"gitDiffs\\\":[],\\\"interpreterResults\\\":[],\\\"images\\\":[],\\\"attachedFolders\\\":[],\\\"attachedFoldersNew\\\":[],\\\"bubbleId\\\":\\\"e39508bd-7fa3-4ada-8c14-2ddd23d6c652\\\",\\\"userResponsesToSuggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"suggestedCodeBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsForCompressingFiles\\\":[],\\\"relevantFiles\\\":[],\\\"toolResults\\\":[],\\\"notepads\\\":[],\\\"capabilities\\\":[],\\\"multiFileLinterErrors\\\":[],\\\"diffHistories\\\":[],\\\"recentLocationsHistory\\\":[],\\\"recentlyViewedFiles\\\":[],\\\"isAgentic\\\":false,\\\"fileDiffTrajectories\\\":[],\\\"existedSubsequentTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"existedPreviousTerminalCommand\\\":false,\\\"docsReferences\\\":[],\\\"webReferences\\\":[],\\\"aiWebSearchResults\\\":[],\\\"requestId\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"attachedFoldersListDirResults\\\":[],\\\"humanChanges\\\":[],\\\"attachedHumanChanges\\\":false,\\\"summarizedComposers\\\":[],\\\"cursorRules\\\":[],\\\"contextPieces\\\":[],\\\"editTrailContexts\\\":[],\\\"allThinkingBlocks\\\":[],\\\"diffsSinceLastApply\\\":[],\\\"deletedFiles\\\":[],\\\"supportedTools\\\":[],\\\"tokenCount\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":0,\\\"outputTokens\\\":0},\\\"attachedFileCodeChunksMetadataOnly\\\":[],\\\"consoleLogs\\\":[],\\\"uiElementPicked\\\":[],\\\"isRefunded\\\":false,\\\"knowledgeItems\\\":[],\\\"documentationSelections\\\":[],\\\"externalLinks\\\":[],\\\"projectLayouts\\\":[],\\\"unifiedMode\\\":2,\\\"capabilityContexts\\\":[],\\\"todos\\\":[],\\\"createdAt\\\":\\\"2026-02-17T10:18:47.023Z\\\",\\\"mcpDescriptors\\\":[],\\\"workspaceUris\\\":[],\\\"conversationState\\\":{\\\"rootPromptMessagesJson\\\":[],\\\"turnsOld\\\":[] =============================================================================\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n-- Email Sequence Enrollments: Deal + Stage Support\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nALTER TABLE email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS deal_id UUID REFERENCES deals(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS stage_id UUID REFERENCES pipeline_stages(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n-- Remove legacy unique constraint (lead_id + sequence_id)\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nALTER TABLE email_sequence_enrollments\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS email_sequence_enrollments_lead_id_sequence_id_key;\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n-- Unique enrollments for lead-based sequences (no deal)\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nCREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS 🧬 DNA Marketing Engine\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nA custom CRM platform with Smart Routing &amp; Intent Detection for the biotech industry.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Features\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- **Event Ingestion**: Receive marketing events from multiple channels (Waalaxy, Lemlist, Website, Ads, Conferences)\\\\\\\\n- **Lead Scoring**: Real-time scoring with configurable rules and decay mechanism\\\\\\\\n- **Intent Detection**: Automatic detection of product intent (Research Lab, B2B Lab, Co-Creation)\\\\\\\\n- **Smart Routing**: Intelligent routing to the correct pipeline based on score + intent\\\\\\\\n- **Automation Engine**: Automated stage movements, notifications, and task creation\\\\\\\\n- **Integrations**: Moco (German finance) and Slack (alerts &amp; notifications)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Tech Stack\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Component | Technology |\\\\\\\\n|-----------|------------|\\\\\\\\n| Runtime | Node.js 20+ |\\\\\\\\n| Framework | Fastify |\\\\\\\\n| Database | PostgreSQL 15+ |\\\\\\\\n| Queue | Redis + BullMQ |\\\\\\\\n| Language | TypeScript (strict) |\\\\\\\\n| Validation | Zod |\\\\\\\\n| Migrations | node-pg-migrate |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Node.js 20+\\\\\\\\n- Docker and Docker Compose\\\\\\\\n- npm v9+\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 1. Clone and Install\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\ngit clone &lt;repository-url&gt;\\\\\\\\ncd dna-marketing-engine\\\\\\\\nnpm install\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 2. Environment Setup\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n# Copy example environment file\\\\\\\\ncp env.example .env\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Edit .env with your configuration\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 3. Start Infrastructure\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n# Start PostgreSQL and Redis\\\\\\\\ndocker-compose up -d\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Wait for services to be healthy\\\\\\\\ndocker-compose ps\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 4. Run Migrations\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n# Apply database migrations\\\\\\\\nnpm run migrate:up\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Or manually:\\\\\\\\ndocker exec -i dna_postgres psql -U dna -d dna_marketing &lt; migrations/1737475200000_initial-schema.sql\\\\\\\\ndocker exec -i dna_postgres psql -U dna -d dna_marketing &lt; migrations/1737475200001_seed-pipelines.sql\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 5. Seed Data (Optional)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\nnpm run seed\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 6. Start Development\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\\\\\\\n# Terminal 1: Start API server with hot reload\\\\\\\\nnpm run dev\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n# Terminal 2: Start background workers\\\\\\\\nnpm run workers\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"signatures\\\\\\\":{},\\\\\\\"detailedLines\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# 🧬 DNA Marketing Engine\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":1},{\\\\\\\"lineNumber\\\\\\\":2},{\\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"A custom CRM platform with Smart Routing &amp; TABLE deals (\\\\\\\\n    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    lead_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES leads(id),\\\\\\\\n    pipeline_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pipelines(id),\\\\\\\\n    stage_id        UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pipeline_stages(id),\\\\\\\\n    name            VARCHAR(255),\\\\\\\\n    value           DECIMAL(12,2),\\\\\\\\n    currency        VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'EUR',\\\\\\\\n    expected_close_date DATE,\\\\\\\\n    stage_entered_at    TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    assigned_to         VARCHAR(255),\\\\\\\\n    assigned_region     VARCHAR(50),\\\\\\\\n    assigned_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    status          VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'open',\\\\\\\\n    close_reason    TEXT,\\\\\\\\n    closed_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    moco_offer_id   VARCHAR(50),\\\\\\\\n    metadata        JSONB DEFAULT '{}',\\\\\\\\n    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    UNIQUE(lead_id, pipeline_id)\\\\\\\\n);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\n-- EVENTS (Partitioned by month)\\\\\\\\n-- =============================================================================\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE events (\\\\\\\\n    id              UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\\\\\\\\n    lead_id         UUID REFERENCES leads(id),\\\\\\\\n    event_type      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    event_category  VARCHAR(50),\\\\\\\\n    source          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    occurred_at     TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\\\\\\\\n    metadata        JSONB DEFAULT '{}',\\\\\\\\n    campaign_id     VARCHAR(100),\\\\\\\\n    utm_source      VARCHAR(100),\\\\\\\\n    utm_medium      VARCHAR(100),\\\\\\\\n    utm_campaign    VARCHAR(100),\\\\\\\\n    correlation_id  UUID,\\\\\\\\n    score_points    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\\\\\\\\n    score_category  VARCHAR(50),\\\\\\\\n    processed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\\\\\n    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\\\\\\\\n    PRIMARY KEY (id, occurred_at)\\\\\\\\n) PARTITION BY RANGE (occurred_at);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n-- Create monthly partitions for 2026\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE events_2026_01 PARTITION OF events FOR VALUES FROM ('2026-01-01') TO ('2026-02-01');\\\\\\\\nCREATE TABLE events_2026_02 PARTITION OF events FOR VALUES DNA ME CRM — Final AWS Deployment Plan\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nMerged from Plans b0ab4d72 and b742a208 with expert review upgrades.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Summary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Aspect | Implementation |\\\\\\\\n|--------|----------------|\\\\\\\\n| **EC2** | 2× t3.medium (App + DB) |\\\\\\\\n| **Region** | eu-central-1 |\\\\\\\\n| **VPC** | 10.0.0.0/16, public subnet only (no NAT) |\\\\\\\\n| **DB** | Public subnet, Public IP, SG-restricted (5432 from App, 22 from AllowedCidr) |\\\\\\\\n| **EBS** | DB volume DeleteOnTermination: false |\\\\\\\\n| **Backup** | Cron 3 AM, local + S3 (IAM role) |\\\\\\\\n| **AllowedCidr** | Default 1.1.1.1/32 (change to your IP) |\\\\\\\\n| **Docker Compose** | Pinned v2.29.1 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Architecture\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\nInternet → Elastic IP → App EC2 (t3.medium)\\\\\\\\n                         ├── Nginx (80/443) + Certbot\\\\\\\\n                         ├── API (:3000)\\\\\\\\n                         ├── Workers\\\\\\\\n                         ├── Frontend (Nginx SPA)\\\\\\\\n                         └── Redis (:6379)\\\\\\\\n                              │ DATABASE_URL=postgres://...@&lt;DB_IP&gt;:5432/...\\\\\\\\n                              ▼\\\\\\\\n                         DB EC2 (t3.medium, private subnet)\\\\\\\\n                         └── Postgres (:5432) in Docker\\\\\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Deploy Sequence (deploy-aws.sh)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. Fetch AppPublicIP and DbPrivateIP from CloudFormation\\\\\\\\n2. Deploy to DB instance first (direct SSH via DbPublicIP or ProxyJump)\\\\\\\\n3. Wait 45s for Postgres to become healthy\\\\\\\\n4. Deploy to App instance (migrate, then docker compose)\\\\\\\\n5. Health check\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Files Created\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| File | Purpose |\\\\\\\\n|------|---------|\\\\\\\\n| &#96;cloudformation.yaml&#96; | VPC, Subnet, IGW, SG, EC2, EIP, User Data |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;docker-compose.aws.yml&#96; | All services, Certbot SSL |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;nginx-aws.conf&#96; | Reverse proxy (HTTP→HTTPS, Frontend, API) |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;nginx-aws.conf.template&#96; | Domain placeholder for custom domains |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;Dockerfile.nginx-certbot&#96; | Nginx + Certbot image |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;entrypoint-nginx-certbot.sh&#96; | SSL init + renewal loop |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;init-letsencrypt.sh&#96; | Dummy certs helper |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;backup.sh&#96; | pg_dump cron script |\\\\\\\\n| &#96;user-data.sh&#96; | EC2 bootstrap (standalone reference) |\\\\\\\\n| Database Migration Setup Summary\\\\n\\\\n### 1. Migration Files in &#96;migrations/&#96; Folder\\\\n\\\\nFound 8 SQL migration files (in chronological order):\\\\n\\\\n1. **&#96;1737475200000_initial-schema.sql&#96;** - Initial schema\\\\n2. **&#96;1737475200001_seed-pipelines.sql&#96;** - Pipeline seed data\\\\n3. **&#96;1737575200000_add_deal_position.sql&#96;** - Adds position column to deals\\\\n4. **&#96;1737675200000_email_sequences.sql&#96;** - Email sequences tables\\\\n5. **&#96;1737975200000_add_stage_color.sql&#96;** - Adds color to pipeline_stages\\\\n6. **&#96;1737980000000_email_sequence_deal_enrollments.sql&#96;** - Adds deal_id and stage_id to enrollments\\\\n7. **&#96;1738000000000_seed_email_sequences.sql&#96;** - Email sequence seed data\\\\n8. **&#96;1771250524235_add_auth_columns.sql&#96;** - Adds auth columns to team_members\\\\n\\\\n### 2. Migration Runner Script\\\\n\\\\n**File:** &#96;scripts/run-migration.ts&#96;\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\\\\nimport { Pool } from 'pg';\\\\nimport dotenv from 'dotenv';\\\\nimport fs from 'fs';\\\\nimport path from 'path';\\\\n\\\\ndotenv.config();\\\\n\\\\nconst pool = new Pool({\\\\n  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,\\\\n});\\\\n\\\\nasync function run() {\\\\n  try {\\\\n    const sqlPath = path.join(process.cwd(), 'migrations', '1771250524235_add_auth_columns.sql');\\\\n    const sql = fs.readFileSync(sqlPath, 'utf8');\\\\n    \\\\n    // Split into Up and Down (simple split by comment if needed, but here I just want Up)\\\\n    const upSql = sql.split('-- Down Migration')[0];\\\\n\\\\n    console.log('Running SQL:', upSql);\\\\n    await pool.query(upSql);\\\\n    console.log('Migration run successfully.');\\\\n\\\\n    // Manually update pgmigrations table\\\\n    const name = '1771250524235_add_auth_columns';\\\\n    await pool.query('INSERT INTO pgmigrations (name, run_on) VALUES ($1, NOW())', [name]);\\\\n    console.log('pgmigrations updated.');\\\\n\\\\n  } catch (e) {\\\\n    console.error(e);\\\\n  } finally {\\\\n    await pool.end();\\\\n  }\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\nrun();\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nNote: This script is hardcoded to run a specific migration (&#96;1771250524235_add_auth_columns.sql&#96;). It manually updates the &#96;pgmigrations&#96; table.\\\\n\\\\n### 3. Package.json Migration Scripts\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\\\\n\\\\\\\"migrate\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"node-pg-migrate\\\\\\\",\\\\n\\\\\\\"migrate:up\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"node-pg-migrate up\\\\\\\",\\\\n\\\\\\\"migrate:down\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"node-pg-migrate down\\\\\\\",\\\\n\\\\\\\"migrate:create\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"node-pg-migrate create\\\\\\\",\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nThe project uses &#96;node-pg-migrate&#96; (v7.0.0) for migrations. The migration tracking table is &#96;pgmigrations&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n### 4. Existing Database Schema - Key Tables\\\\n\\\\nFrom &#96;1737475200000_initial-schema.sql&#96;:\\\\n\\\\n#### Core Tables with Foreign Keys:\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;leads&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;organization_id&#96; → &#96;organizations(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;pipeline_id&#96; → &#96;pipelines(id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;deals&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96; (NOT NULL)\\\\n- &#96;pipeline_id&#96; → &#96;pipelines(id)&#96; (NOT NULL)\\\\n- &#96;stage_id&#96; → &#96;pipeline_stages(id)&#96; (NOT NULL)\\\\n- Unique constraint: &#96;UNIQUE(lead_id, pipeline_id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;team_members&#96;** table:\\\\n- No foreign keys (standalone table)\\\\n- Columns: &#96;id&#96;, &#96;email&#96; (UNIQUE), &#96;name&#96;, &#96;role&#96;, &#96;region&#96;, &#96;is_active&#96;, &#96;max_leads&#96;, &#96;current_leads&#96;, &#96;created_at&#96;\\\\n- Auth columns added in migration &#96;1771250524235_add_auth_columns.sql&#96;: &#96;password_hash&#96;, &#96;two_factor_secret&#96;, &#96;is_two_factor_enabled&#96;, &#96;temp_two_factor_secret&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;pipeline_stages&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;pipeline_id&#96; → &#96;pipelines(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n- Unique constraints: &#96;UNIQUE(pipeline_id, slug)&#96;, &#96;UNIQUE(pipeline_id, position)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;intent_signals&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;score_history&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n- &#96;rule_id&#96; → &#96;scoring_rules(id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;automation_rules&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;pipeline_id&#96; → &#96;pipelines(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;stage_id&#96; → &#96;pipeline_stages(id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;automation_logs&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;rule_id&#96; → &#96;automation_rules(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;deal_id&#96; → &#96;deals(id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;tasks&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;deal_id&#96; → &#96;deals(id)&#96;\\\\n- &#96;automation_rule_id&#96; → &#96;automation_rules(id)&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;email_sequence_enrollments&#96;** table (from &#96;1737675200000_email_sequences.sql&#96;):\\\\n- &#96;lead_id&#96; → &#96;leads(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n- &#96;sequence_id&#96; → &#96;email_sequences(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n- &#96;deal_id&#96; → &#96;deals(id)&#96; ON DELETE SET NULL (added in &#96;1737980000000_email_sequence_deal_enrollments.sql&#96;)\\\\n- &#96;stage_id&#96; → &#96;pipeline_stages(id)&#96; ON DELETE SET NULL (added in &#96;1737980000000_email_sequence_deal_enrollments.sql&#96;)\\\\n\\\\n**&#96;email_tracking&#96;** table:\\\\n- &#96;enrollment_id&#96; → &#96;email_sequence_enrollments(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n- &#96;step_id&#96; → &#96;email_sequence_steps(id)&#96; ON DELETE CASCADE\\\\n\\\\n### 5. Migrations Backup Folder\\\\n\\\\nThe &#96;migrations_backup/&#96; folder contains 8 files (same as &#96;migrations/&#96;):\\\\n- Same filenames and content\\\\n- Appears to be a backup copy\\\\n\\\\n### Full Migration File Contents\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 1: &#96;1737475200000_initial-schema.sql&#96;\\\\nCreates:\\\\n- &#96;organizations&#96;\\\\n- &#96;pipelines&#96;\\\\n- &#96;pipeline_stages&#96;\\\\n- &#96;leads&#96; (with scoring and routing fields)\\\\n- &#96;intent_signals&#96;\\\\n- &#96;deals&#96;\\\\n- &#96;events&#96; (partitioned by month)\\\\n- &#96;scoring_rules&#96;\\\\n- &#96;score_history&#96;\\\\n- &#96;automation_rules&#96;\\\\n- &#96;automation_logs&#96;\\\\n- &#96;tasks&#96;\\\\n- &#96;team_members&#96;\\\\n- &#96;campaigns&#96;\\\\n- &#96;api_keys&#96;\\\\n\\\\nIncludes indexes, helper functions (&#96;recalculate_lead_scores&#96;, &#96;expire_old_scores&#96;, &#96;update_updated_at&#96;), and triggers.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 2: &#96;1737475200001_seed-pipelines.sql&#96;\\\\nSeeds 4 pipelines:\\\\n- Discovery Pipeline (default)\\\\n- Research Lab Pipeline\\\\n- B2B Lab Enablement Pipeline\\\\n- Panel Co-Creation Pipeline\\\\n\\\\nPlus their stages.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 3: &#96;1737575200000_add_deal_position.sql&#96;\\\\nAdds &#96;position&#96; INTEGER column to &#96;deals&#96; with default 0, populates existing rows, and creates index.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 4: &#96;1737675200000_email_sequences.sql&#96;\\\\nCreates:\\\\n- &#96;email_sequences&#96;\\\\n- &#96;email_sequence_steps&#96;\\\\n- &#96;email_sequence_enrollments&#96;\\\\n- &#96;email_tracking&#96;\\\\n\\\\nIncludes helper functions and triggers.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 5: &#96;1737975200000_add_stage_color.sql&#96;\\\\nAdds &#96;color VARCHAR(7)&#96; to &#96;pipeline_stages&#96;.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 6: &#96;1737980000000_email_sequence_deal_enrollments.sql&#96;\\\\nAdds &#96;deal_id&#96; and &#96;stage_id&#96; to &#96;email_sequence_enrollments&#96;, removes old unique constraint, and creates new unique indexes.\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 7: &#96;1738000000000_seed_email_sequences.sql&#96;\\\\nSeeds 5 email sequences with multiple steps each (22 total steps).\\\\n\\\\n#### Migration 8: &#96;1771250524235_add_auth_columns.sql&#96;\\\\nAdds auth columns to &#96;team_members&#96;:\\\\n- &#96;password_hash TEXT&#96;\\\\n- &#96;two_factor_secret TEXT&#96;\\\\n- &#96;is_two_factor_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE&#96;\\\\n- &#96;temp_two_factor_secret TEXT&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Summary\\\\n\\\\n- Migration system: Uses &#96;node-pg-migrate&#96; with SQL files\\\\n- Migration tracking: &#96;pgmigrations&#96; table\\\\n- Custom runner: &#96;scripts/run-migration.ts&#96; (hardcoded for one migration)\\\\n- Foreign key relationships: Well-defined relationships between &#96;leads&#96;, &#96;deals&#96;, &#96;pipelines&#96;, &#96;pipeline_stages&#96;, &#96;team_members&#96;, and related tables\\\\n- Schema evolution: Migrations add features incrementally (email sequences, deal positions, auth, etc.)\\\\n\\\\nThe schema supports a CRM with lead scoring, intent detection, pipeline management, email sequences, and team member Die Datenbank-Migration ist vollständig implementiert. Hier eine Zusammenfassung der Änderungen:

---

### Neue Datei: `migrations/1771350000000_add_chat_system.sql`

Erstellt **4 neue Tabellen** gemäß dem Plan:

1. **`conversations`** – Zentrale Tabelle für Chat-Konversationen
   - Foreign Keys zu `leads`, `deals`, `team_members`
   - CHECK-Constraint: mindestens `lead_id` oder `deal_id` muss gesetzt sein
   - CHECK-Constraints für `type` und `status`
   - JSONB-Feld `participant_emails` für Teilnehmer

2. **`messages`** – Alle Nachrichtentypen (E-Mail, LinkedIn, Notizen, Aufgaben)
   - `sender_id` (FK zu `team_members`) für internen Absender
   - `email_thread_id` für E-Mail-Thread-Matching (In-Reply-To)
   - `status` mit Sendequeue-Tracking (`draft` → `sending` → `sent`/`error`)
   - `error_message` für Fehlermeldungen
   - JSONB-Felder: `recipients`, `metadata`, `attachments`

3. **`email_accounts`** – IMAP/SMTP-Konfiguration pro Team-Mitglied

4. **`linkedin_connections`** – LinkedIn OAuth-Token und Profilverknüpfung

### Indexes erstellt:

- **13 B-Tree-Indexes** für häufige Abfragen (inkl. partieller Index auf `messages.status` für `sending`/`error`)
- **Unique Index** auf `messages.external_id` (WHERE NOT NULL) für Deduplizierung
- **5 GIN-Indexes** für JSONB-Felder:
  - `conversations.participant_emails`
  - `messages.recipients`
  - `messages.metadata`
  - `messages.attachments`
  - `linkedin_connections.profile_data`

### Weitere Änderungen:

- **`scripts/run-migration.ts`** – Akzeptiert jetzt ein Migrations-Dateiname als CLI-Argument und prüft auf bereits angewendete Migrationen
- **`src/types/index.ts`** – Neue TypeScript-Interfaces (`Conversation`, `Message`, `EmailAccount`, `LinkedInConnection`) und zugehörige Union-Types hinzugefügt

### Ausführen der Migration:

```bash
npx tsx scripts/run-migration.ts 1771350000000_add_chat_system.sql
```

**Nein, am Deploy-Skript muss nichts geändert werden.** Alles ist bereits korrekt konfiguriert:

1. **`migrations/`-Ordner wird mitgepackt** (Zeile 130 im Skript):

```130:130:deploy/aws/deploy-aws-ssm.sh
  migrations/ \
```

Die neue Datei `1771350000000_add_chat_system.sql` wird automatisch im `tar`-Archiv eingeschlossen.

2. **Migrationen werden automatisch ausgeführt** (Zeile 200):

```200:200:deploy/aws/deploy-aws-ssm.sh
sudo -u ec2-user bash -c 'cd /opt/dna-crm && npm ci && export \$(grep -v ^# .env.aws 2>/dev/null | xargs) && npm run migrate:up'
```

`npm run migrate:up` ruft `node-pg-migrate up` auf, das automatisch alle neuen SQL-Dateien im `migrations/`-Ordner erkennt und in der richtigen Reihenfolge (nach Timestamp) ausführt. Die Datei `migrate-config.json` zeigt auf `migrationsDir: "migrations"`.

3. **`node-pg-migrate`** verwaltet die `pgmigrations`-Tabelle und merkt sich, welche Migrationen bereits gelaufen sind. `1771350000000_add_chat_system.sql` wird beim nächsten Deploy automatisch als neue Migration erkannt und ausgeführt.

**Zusammenfassung**: Einfach `bash deploy/aws/deploy-aws-ssm.sh` ausführen — die Chat-System-Migration wird automatisch beim Deployment angewendet.
